title: Customs Management and Duties (Amendment) Act, 2023
text:
No. 18 of 2023
VIRGIN ISLANDS
CUSTOMS MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES
(AMENDMENT) ACT, 2023
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title and commencement
2. New Part XIIA inserted
1

No. 18 of 2023 Customs Management and Duties Virgin
(Amendment) Act, 2023 Islands
I ASSENT
(Sgd.) John Rankin CMG,
Governor.
27th March, 2023
VIRGIN ISLANDS
No. 18 of 2023
AN ACT TO AMEND THE CUSTOMS MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES ACT,
2010, NO. 6 OF 2010, AND TO PROVIDE FOR MATTERS CONCERNING
THEREWITH.
[Gazetted 28th March, 2023]
ENACTED by the Legislature of the Virgin Islands as follows:
Short title and commencement
1. (1) This Act may be cited as the Customs Management and Duties
(Amendment) Act, 2023.
(2) This Act shall come into force on such date as the Minister may, by
Notice published in the Gazette, appoint.
New Part XIIA inserted
2. The Customs Management and Duties Act, 2010 (No. 6 of 2010), is
amended by inserting immediately after section 142, the following new Part:
“PART XIIA
COOPERATION WITH LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES,
COMPETENT AUTHORITIES AND FOREIGN LAW
ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES
Duty to cooperate
142A. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Act, the Commissioner may,
upon request in writing or on his own volition, co-operate with:
(a) any law enforcement agency in the Territory;
(b) a competent authority acting pursuant to an enactment;
or
(c) any foreign law enforcement agency,
for the purposes of
2

(i) investigating an offence;
(ii) assisting with or facilitating any investigating of
an offence; or
(iii) ensuring compliance with a request from a law
enforcement agency in the Territory, a competent
authority or a foreign law enforcement agency,
acting pursuant to an enactment.
(2) Co-operation for the purposes of this section may include
(a) the sharing of documents and information which the
Commissioner is not prevented by this Act or any other
enactment from disclosing; or
(b) making requests for assistance to any law enforcement
agency in the Territory, competent authority or foreign
law enforcement agency acting pursuant to an
enactment.
(3) Where, pursuant to a request for assistance made in
accordance with this section, any information, document or other
material is received, the Commissioner shall, if requested in writing by
the authority that provided the information, document or other material,
provide in a timely manner feedback to that authority on the use and
usefulness of the information, document or other material received.
(4) The Minister upon consultation with the Commissioner
may issue such guidelines as may be required for the procedures and
measures in relation to cooperation with law enforcement agencies,
competent authorities and foreign law enforcement agencies for the
purposes of this section.
(5) For the purposes of this section, “foreign law
enforcement agencies” means any authority in a jurisdiction outside the
Virgin Islands which exercises a power or performs a function
corresponding or similar to a power or function exercised or performed
by Customs, as established under the Customs Management and Duties
Act, 2010, No. 6 of 2010.”.
Passed by the House of Assembly this 10th day of March, 2023.
(Sgd.) Corine N. George-Massicote,
Speaker.
(Sgd.) Phyllis Evans,
Clerk of the House of Assembly.
3
